config.systemPrompt:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

methodology: One read-only external-project analysis task executed through a DeepSeek LLM agent; it is operationally measured but not success-scored.

with_caesar: LLM has access to 18 CAESAR graph tools (search_graph, trace_path, explain_symbol, find_tests, get_architecture, analyze_hotspots, get_graph_schema, semantic_search, batch_get_code, search_code, smart_review, detect_changes, pack_memory, query_graph, compare_runs, pack_context, get_code_snippet, read_file).
without_caesar: LLM has access to read_file + grep only.
Fresh conversation per task x condition x run. No shared history or cache between conditions.
Paid microbenchmark protocol: exact deepseek-v4-flash, temperature 0, fixed per-round seeds, identical prompts/order/limits/runtime; no model or provider fallback.
Mode: live API calls. Suite: external-project.
Cost is estimated from token usage and configured pricing. Wall time includes API latency.
ROI claims blocked when baseline invalid or sample size too small.

messages.content:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

I'm doing a source cleanup sprint. Find symbols (functions, classes) in the codebase that appear to have zero callers and zero usages — these are candidates for removal. List at least 5 concrete examples with their file paths and names. Do NOT invent symbols. Only report symbols you have verified are defined in the codebase. Respond with JSON: {"unused_symbols":[{"name":"...","file":"...","kind":"function|class"}], "evidence":["..."], "summary":"..."}.

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments:
{"query": "MATCH (m:Method)
WHERE NOT (m)<-[:CALLS]-(:Method)
  AND NOT (m)<-[:CALLS]-(:File)
  AND NOT (m)<-[:TESTS]-(:Method)
RETURN m.name AS name, m.parentClass AS className, m.lineCount AS lineCount
ORDER BY m.lineCount DESC"}

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments:
{"query": "MATCH (c:Class)
WHERE NOT (c)<-[:READS]-(:Method)
  AND NOT (c)<-[:READS]-(:File)
  AND NOT (c)<-[:DEFINES]-(:File)
RETURN c.name AS name, c.lineCount AS lineCount"}

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments: {"query": "MATCH (m:Method {name: \"generateMarkdown\"})\nOPTIONAL MATCH (m)<-[r:CALLS]-()\nRETURN m.name, count(r) AS incomingCallCount, collect(labels(startNode(r))) AS callerLabels"}

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments: {"query": "SELECT n.name, n.qualified_name, n.file_path, n.kind FROM nodes n WHERE n.kind IN ('Method', 'Class') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM edges e WHERE (e.target_id = n.id) AND e.type IN ('CALLS', 'TESTS')) LIMIT 50"}

evidence: DeepTheme.glowColor(for:) — Static method (lines 70-77, Theme.swift) returns a mode-tinted glow color. Zero CALLS edges in the graph. Zero text matches across all .swift files via grep. Not used in any SwiftUI view.

AppState.getProviderForMode(_:) — Method (lines 300-306, AppState.swift) maps a Mode enum to a provider string. Zero incoming CALLS edges. Zero grep matches. Note: getCurrentProvider() (lines 295-299) does the same thing and IS called — this is a dead duplicate.

ContextExporter.extractFirstRealUserMessage(from:) — Static method (lines 242-248, ContextExporter.swift) filters user messages to find the first real query. Zero incoming CALLS edges. Zero grep matches. Never referenced by generateMarkdown or any other method.

AppState.openAccessibilitySettings() — One-line pass-through (line 258, AppState.swift) delegating to alertManager.openAccessibilitySettings(). Zero CALLS edges. Zero grep matches. Not wired to any button action in ContentView.

CompletionAlertManager.openAccessibilitySettings() — Method (lines 207-208, CompletionAlertManager.swift) opens macOS Accessibility settings pane. Only reachable via AppState.openAccessibilitySettings() which is itself unreachable. Zero CALLS edges. Zero grep matches.
result.summary: Found 5 concrete unused symbols after cross-referencing the code knowledge graph (zero incoming CALLS edges via trace_path), full-text grep (zero matches across the codebase for each symbol name), and manual source code review of all files. Two of the symbols (AppState.openAccessibilitySettings and CompletionAlertManager.openAccessibilitySettings) form an orphaned delegation chain — neither is reachable. getProviderForMode is a dead duplicate of the actively-used getCurrentProvider() method with identical logic. glowColor and extractFirstRealUserMessage are standalone methods that were likely written but never integrated into any UI or data flow.

messages.content:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

I'm doing a source cleanup sprint. Find symbols (functions, classes) in the codebase that appear to have zero callers and zero usages — these are candidates for removal. List at least 5 concrete examples with their file paths and names. Do NOT invent symbols. Only report symbols you have verified are defined in the codebase. Respond with JSON: {"unused_symbols":[{"name":"...","file":"...","kind":"function|class"}], "evidence":["..."], "summary":"..."}.

evidence: ContextExporter.extractFirstRealUserMessage (ContextExporter.swift:242) is defined as a public static function but never called anywhere; the file shows only its definition line and no callers in any other source file.

AppState.getProviderForMode (AppState.swift:300) is a public instance method but has zero callers outside its definition; only getCurrentProvider (a different method) is used in ContinueWorkModal.swift and AppState.swift.
RoutingInfo.providerCount (AdvancedCapabilities.swift:161) is a computed var that only appears in its definition; it is never read by any view or other code.
DeepTheme.windowWidth (Theme.swift:80) and windowHeight (Theme.swift:81) are defined as static constants but the codebase never references DeepTheme.windowWidth or DeepTheme.windowHeight anywhere.

DeepTheme.cornerRadiusLarge/cornerRadiusMedium/cornerRadiusSmall (Theme.swift:82-84) are static constants defined but never referenced; all corner radii in the codebase are hardcoded literals (e.g. 10, 12, 14, 16).
result.summary: Found 8 symbols with zero callers or usages across the DeepCodex Swift codebase. Two are public instance/static methods (extractFirstRealUserMessage, getProviderForMode) that were likely written in anticipation of future use but never wired up. One computed property on RoutingInfo (providerCount) is defined but never consumed. Five static layout constants in DeepTheme (windowWidth, windowHeight, cornerRadiusLarge, cornerRadiusMedium, cornerRadiusSmall) are defined but all view layout uses hardcoded values instead. All are safe removal candidates.
